rationale: AB 2013 requires tracking data sources. This control mandates the inventory of all training datasets (provenance, copyright status, PI presence) and the tagging of data assets, serving as the source of truth for the required public summary.

The law requires a high-level summary be posted publicly. This control governs the approval and publication of transparency artifacts, ensuring the disclosure is legally sufficient without revealing trade secrets.
SB 942 requires embedded metadata (watermarking). This control operationalizes the technical requirement to embed provenance signals into model outputs (images/video/audio) to prove origin.

You must disclose if training data contains PI or IP. This control enforces the classification of raw data inputs before training, enabling the organization to accurately answer the statutory question: Does this contain personal info?

Providers must revoke licenses of bad actors within 96 hours. This control establishes the contractual right to audit and terminate third-party licensees who strip watermarks or violate safety policies.
Providers must offer a public detection tool. This control mandates the development and maintenance of a verification utility that allows external users to authenticate content provenance.

Disclosure of copyrighted training data is mandatory. This control ensures the legal review of data ingest pipelines to characterize IP risks, ensuring the public disclosure does not inadvertently admit to infringement.
Watermarks must be difficult to remove. This control integrates adversarial testing into the SDLC to verify that provenance markers are robust against common tampering techniques.
